free_text: Comparison of measured and calculated mass
distributions of fission fragments in proton-induced
fission of 232Th, 235U, 238U, and 237Np at
intermediate energies
Gustaf Werner cyclotron
No information
The fission fragments passed through a pair
of microchannel plate detectors (MCP) placed on
either side of the target at 90 degree with respect
to the beam.
Electrons, knocked out by fission fragments in passing
through aluminum oxide foils (first foil: 60 mu-g/cm2,
15 mm in diam., located at distance of 10 mm from the
target, second foil: 60 mu-g/cm2, 30 mm in diam.,
located at distance of 80 mm from the target), were
focused on MCPs.
After passing through the time-of-flight arm, fission
fragments could also strike silicon detectors (30 mm in
diam., 80 mm distance from the targ which were used to
measure the fragment energies and to act as stop
detectors for the time-of-flight measurement.
No information on source of uncertainty
Data in Fig.1 received from Dr.O.Batenkov
